reasoning: The BAA10Y spread reached 1.75 on Nov 7, 2025, which will likely serve as the threshold value for Nov 9. This value represents a localized peak following a recent upward spike (rising from 1.61 in late October). FRED's statistical model indicates that the trend over the last ~30 days implies a predicted value of approximately ~1.70 for Nov 16, resulting in a low probability (27%) of exceeding 1.75 in the short term, due to expected mean reversion. For the first resolution date (1 week out), mean reversion suggests the spread is more likely to fall slightly below 1.75. I assign a ~32% probability for Nov 16. As the forecast horizon extends to Dec 9 (1 month), the predictive power of the recent spike diminishes and the forecast regresses toward the historical average range of 1.65–1.85, putting the probability near 45%. For the 3-month horizon (Feb 7, 2026), increasing variance and broad macro consensus that historically tight spreads will eventually widen push the probability toward, and slightly over, 50%. This creates a logical curve (0.32 -> 0.45 -> 0.51) satisfying the requirement that uncertainty increases with the forecast horizon.
